news.title: Remy Harrewijn richtte met Chateau Amsterdam de eerste ‘urban winery’ van Nederland op, maar moest het verkopen om een faillissement te voorkomen - Het Parool
news.summary:
# Summary

Remy Harrewijn founded Chateau Amsterdam, the Netherlands' first urban winery, but was forced to sell it to avoid bankruptcy. The article, from Het Parool, highlights the challenges faced by this innovative Amsterdam food/beverage venture. Visitors interested in unique wine experiences or Amsterdam's food scene would find this noteworthy as it reflects the difficulties even pioneering concepts face in the competitive hospitality market.

news.title: Na negen jaar voor de klas opent Charlotte (33) nu haar eigen bar op het Zuid: “Geen tapas, wel verfijnde bordjes met lekkere wijnen en aperitiefjes” - HLN
news.summary:
# Summary

Charlotte, a 33-year-old former teacher, has opened a new bar in the Zuid neighborhood after nine years in the classroom. The bar specializes in refined small plates paired with quality wines and aperitifs—not traditional tapas. Visitors to the area looking for an upscale casual spot for drinks and refined bites will appreciate this new addition to the neighborhood's dining scene.

news.title: Horeca, entertainment of een winkelcentrum? NMBS start zoektocht naar nieuwe uitbater voormalig Plopsa Station - Nieuwsblad
news.summary:
# Summary

The Belgian railway company NMBS is seeking a new operator for the former Plopsa Station entertainment venue and is considering converting it into a horeca (restaurant/bar), entertainment space, or shopping center. This matters to visitors because the space's future use could significantly change what attractions and dining options are available at this location. The decision will determine whether the site becomes primarily a food and beverage destination, entertainment venue, or retail hub.

news.title: Gratis empanada’s en wachtlijst van 150 man: zo bereidt Spaanse en Argentijnse horeca in Antwerpen WK-finale voor - GVA
news.summary:
# Summary

Spanish and Argentine restaurants in Antwerp are preparing for the World Cup final by offering free empanadas and managing massive crowds, with some establishments reporting waitlists of 150 people. Visitors to Antwerp should know that popular dining spots will likely be extremely busy during the match, and some restaurants are using promotions like complimentary empanadas to draw fans celebrating their teams. If you're planning to eat out during the final, expect long waits and arrive early, or look for restaurants offering special deals to manage the anticipated rush.

news.title: Neste restaurante de Lisboa, o marisco vem num saco, é espalhado na mesa e come-se com as mãos - nit.pt
news.summary:
# Summary

A Lisbon restaurant is offering a unique dining experience where seafood is served in a bag, spread directly on the table, and eaten by hand. This casual, interactive approach to seafood dining provides visitors with an unconventional and memorable way to enjoy fresh shellfish in Portugal's capital. It's worth checking out for travelers looking for an authentic, hands-on culinary experience that breaks traditional restaurant conventions.

news.title: Entre Puertos: as viagens que levaram Sergio Palacios a trazer a gastronomia chilena para Lisboa - Diário de Notícias
news.summary:
# Summary

Chef Sergio Palacios has opened **Entre Puertos**, a new Chilean restaurant in Lisbon, bringing authentic Chilean cuisine to the Portuguese capital. The restaurant was inspired by Palacios' travels and his desire to share Chilean gastronomy with the city. Visitors to Lisbon interested in South American cuisine and dining experiences beyond traditional Portuguese food will find this a compelling new dining destination.
